sourceMovie.title: Stripes
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Stripes works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The A-Team
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Stripes clicked for you, The A-Team (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Stripes?
faq.answer: Try The A-Team, Johnny English Strikes Again, Hot Shots!—each shares DNA with Stripes while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Stripes?
faq.answer: Stripes (1981) is best described as Action, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Stripes worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
